content_markdown: The acceleration of free fall of a mass of $2.0\text{ kg}$ close to the surface of Mars is $3.6\text{ m s}^{-2}$. What is the gravitational field strength at the surface of Mars in $\text{N kg}^{-1}$?
vibe_explanation: The gravitational field strength $g$ is numerically equal to the acceleration of free fall at that location. Since the acceleration is given as $3.6\text{ m s}^{-2}$, the field strength is $3.6\text{ N kg}^{-1}$. The mass of the object ($2.0\text{ kg}$) is a distractor because field strength is a property of the location, not the object placed there.
